name: style-diff-discrimination
prompt: /rpiv:deep-review commit e80409c — an automated sqlfluff style pass over the SQL migrations; want to be sure it changed nothing behavioral. (Headless non-interactive run: at any approval checkpoint or question, pick the recommended option and continue; never wait for user input.)
ground_truth_note: Originally designed as a clean-diff precision eval — but the baseline run found, and `atlas migrate validate` CONFIRMED, that e80409c reformatted all migrations without re-running `atlas migrate hash`: atlas.sum is stale at HEAD (checksum error, live in the real repo). So the commit hides exactly ONE real tooling breakage. Correct review = that one blocker and nothing else: recall the atlas.sum staleness, keep total findings bounded (the 47-file style diff itself is behavior-preserving).
